title: Boat and ship builders and repairers
description: Perform engineering duties in designing, constructing, and testing aircraft, missiles, and spacecraft. May conduct basic and applied research to evaluate adaptability of materials and equipment to aircraft design and manufacture. May recommend improvements in testing equipment and techniques. Apply knowledge of engineering technology and biological science to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery, electrification, structures, soil and water conservation, and processing of agricultural products. Design, develop, and evaluate the operation of marine vessels, ship machinery, and related equipment, such as power supply and propulsion systems. (Based on 44 related US occupations.)

Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards
Judging the Qualities of Objects, Services, or People
Operating Vehicles, Mechanized Devices, or Equipment

Providing Consultation and Advice to Others
Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others
Communicating with People Outside the Organization
Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

tasks.task: Release air bubbles and smooth seams, using rollers.
Trim cured materials by sawing them with diamond-impregnated cutoff wheels.
Erect scaffolding or ladders for assembling structures above ground level.
Remove damaged or defective parts or sections of structures and repair or replace, using hand tools.
Install rough door and window frames, subflooring, fixtures, or temporary supports in structures undergoing construction or repair.
Inspect ceiling or floor tile, wall coverings, siding, glass, or woodwork to detect broken or damaged structures.
Assemble and fasten materials to make frameworks or props, using hand tools and wood screws, nails, dowel pins, or glue.
Trim excess materials from molds, using hand shears or trimming knives.
Check completed products for conformance to specifications and for defects by measuring with rulers or micrometers, by checking them visually, or by tapping them to detect bubbles or dead spots.
Bond wood reinforcing strips to decks and cabin structures of watercraft, using resin-saturated fiberglass.
Mix catalysts into resins, and saturate cloth and mats with mixtures, using brushes.
Observe meters and gauges to verify and record temperatures, pressures, and press-cycle times.
Smooth workpiece edges and fix taps, tubes, and valves.
Set up and operate fabricating machines, such as brakes, rolls, shears, flame cutters, grinders, and drill presses, to bend, cut, form, punch, drill, or otherwise form and assemble metal components.
Verify vertical and horizontal alignment of structural steel members, using plumb bobs, laser equipment, transits, or levels.
Dismantle structures or equipment.
Lift or move materials and finished products, using large cranes.
Verify trueness of structure, using plumb bob and level.
Fabricate metal parts, such as steel frames, columns, beams, or girders, according to blueprints or instructions from supervisors.
Inspect coated or plated areas for defects, such as air bubbles or uneven coverage.
Adjust dials to regulate flow of current and voltage supplied to terminals to control plating processes.

Assist engineers to design, develop, test, or manufacture industrial machinery, consumer products, or other equipment, and provide technical support regarding mechanical design, fabrication, testing, or documentation. Prepare layouts of machinery, tools, plants, or equipment, and prepare specifications, designs, or sketches for machines, components, or systems related to the generation, transmission, or use of mechanical or fluid energy. Read and interpret blueprints, schematics, or drawings to determine methods and sequences of assembly.

Test machines, components, materials, or products to determine characteristics such as performance, strength, or response to stress; document test results and analyze test results in relation to design or specifications. Conduct failure analyses, document results, and recommend corrective actions.

Assemble or disassemble complex mechanical systems and equipment, installing parts or systems to be tested in test equipment, using hand tools and power tools; modify, maintain, or repair electrical, electronic, or mechanical components, equipment, or systems to ensure proper functioning.

Inspect, diagnose, maintain, and operate test setups and equipment to detect malfunctions; inspect parts for surface defects, verify part dimensions to ensure conformance to specifications, and examine parts for damage or excessive wear using precision measuring instruments.

Fabricate or assemble mechanical, electrical, or electronic components or assemblies, including needed parts from sheet metal, and weld or solder broken parts and structural members to ensure structural integrity. Clean parts and perform routine maintenance work on equipment, and order new test equipment, supplies, or replacement parts as necessary.

Monitor, inspect, or test mechanical equipment, and perform preventive or corrective maintenance on robotic systems or components. Modify computer-controlled robot movements to meet specified performance standards and test performance of robotic assemblies using appropriate instrumentation.

Analyze performance of vehicles or components that have been redesigned to increase fuel efficiency, and recommend product or component design improvements based on test data or observations; engage in statistical studies to analyze production costs for sustainable designs.
Manage project instructions and specifications to identify fabrication, assembly, and testing requirements; document robotics test procedures and results to uphold quality assurance.
Train customers or other personnel to install, use, or maintain robots and facilitate the training of robots using artificial intelligence techniques.
Dismantle and reassemble heavy equipment and machinery using hoists and hand tools; diagnose faults or malfunctions using diagnostic equipment to determine necessary repairs.
Estimate cost factors including labor and material for purchased and fabricated parts during assembly, testing, or installation.

Set up mechanical, hydraulic, or electric test equipment in accordance with engineering specifications and standards; prepare equipment inspection schedules or other records necessary for operational efficiency.
Compile and maintain service records of robotic equipment or automated production systems for inventory and requisition management of machine parts, equipment, and other supplies.

Participate in the design, configuration, or application of robotic systems; evaluate efficiency and reliability of industrial robotic systems, reprogramming or calibrating to maximize quantity and quality.
Evaluate engineering designs for compliance with environmental standards and conduct statistical analyses of sustainable and non-sustainable designs to enhance production methods.
Read production schedules to determine setups of equipment and machines needed for fabrication and assembly processes.
Identify energy-conserving production or fabrication methods that enhance efficiency and reduce waste during assembly processes.
Schedule maintenance for industrial machines and equipment to uphold operational standards and effectiveness.

Read work orders and specifications to determine machines and equipment requiring repair or maintenance. Set up and operate machines, adjusting controls as necessary, and collaborate with other workers to repair or move machines or equipment. Inspect or test damaged machine parts, and mark defective areas or advise supervisors of repair needs. Collect and discard worn machine parts and other refuse to maintain machinery and work areas. Clean machine parts using various methods, and replace or repair components in machinery or equipment. Remove hardened material, lubricate machines, and start machines to observe operations for efficiency and detect problems.

Transport machine parts and tools between work areas and storage using cranes or dollies. Adjust repaired or replaced components as needed to ensure proper operation. Reassemble equipment after inspections, and document repairs and maintenance performed.

Examine parts for defects, troubleshoot equipment malfunctions using testing devices, and record operational data to determine the necessity of adjustments or repairs. Analyze test results and machine error messages to diagnose equipment issues. Conduct preventative maintenance and troubleshoot mechanical, hydraulic, or electrical malfunctions.

Operate newly repaired machinery to verify repairs, and demonstrate equipment functions to operators. Study blueprints and manufacturers' manuals to determine installation or operation specifications. Record parts or materials used and order new parts as necessary.

Fabricate, repair, and install parts, tools, or equipment, including using machine tools such as lathes and grinders. Dismantle and reassemble machinery and equipment to perform repairs or prepare for shipment. Perform routine machine maintenance and ensure machines are properly lubricated or cooled.

Instruct new workers in machine operation, and train end-users or technicians in technical procedures and repairs. Ensure tool and spare parts inventories are maintained for repair and installation services.
Dispose of scrap or waste material in accordance with regulations, and separate scrap waste for reuse or recycling. Maintain machine tools and ensure they are in proper operational condition.

Repair, rewire, and troubleshoot electrical systems using hand tools and testing equipment, and replace defective wiring as necessary. Analyze, test, and document conditions of machinery or electrical components to determine operational efficiency, and keep records of repairs.
Align machines or equipment using various tools to ensure proper functioning, and set machine stops or guides. Adjust controls to maintain temperatures and heating times as necessary.

Weld, repair, and fabricate machinery or mechanical components using various tools and equipment. Align or mount workpieces in machines for fabrication and inspect for dimensional accuracy or defects throughout the machining process.

Analyze machining procedures for improved efficiency and evaluate the operational specifications of designs. Design fixtures or tooling to meet engineering needs or prepare sketches for product illustration.

Perform disassembly and assembly of parts in machinery or vehicles, and ensure compliance with specifications and tolerances. Maintain accurate documentation for repairs, maintenance, and modifications of machinery or equipment.

Contact customers or conference with clients to assess machinery performance and receive feedback necessary for repair or improvements. Conduct inspections on machinery and report to supervisors on conditions observed.

Conduct manual and mechanical operations to adjust, dismantle, or repair machinery while following safety standards and protocols. Collaborate with engineers or other technicians in conducting inspections or testing according to plans.

Optimize fabrication processes and recommend modifications to improve adaptability of machinery or designs. Participate in research activities to evaluate new materials or equipment methodologies in relation to design.

Perform specialized tasks related to marine vessels, ship machinery, and equipment, ensuring that design specifications and performance evaluations meet regulatory standards, and integrate new technologies or sustainability practices as they emerge.
Fit and assemble parts to make or repair machine tools and equipment. Dismantle machines or equipment to examine parts for defects and replace defective parts where needed.

Move controls or mount gears, cams, or templates in machines to set feed rates and cutting speeds, depths, and angles. Align layout marks with dies or blades. Measure and mark reference points and cutting lines on workpieces using tools such as templates, compasses, and rules.

Support metalworking projects from planning and fabrication through assembly, inspection, and testing, using knowledge of machine functions, metal properties, and mathematics. Conduct research that tests or analyzes the feasibility, design, operation, or performance of equipment, components, or systems. Test marine machinery and equipment to ensure adequate performance.

Confer with engineering, supervisory, or manufacturing personnel to exchange technical information. Confer with numerical control programmers to ensure that new programs or machinery function properly and output meets specifications.
Program computers or electronic instruments for machine tools and operations. Write technical reports or proposals related to engineering projects.

Compute data, such as gear dimensions or machine settings, applying knowledge of shop mathematics. Conduct analytical, environmental, operational, or performance studies to develop designs for products, such as marine engines, equipment, and structures.

Validate designs of components or systems. Design or analyze marine vessels, ship machinery, and related equipment such as power supply and propulsion systems, using computer-aided design (CAD) technology. Design complete hull and superstructure according to specifications and test data, in conformity with safety, efficiency, and economy standards.
Write performance requirements for product development or engineering projects. Prepare plans, estimates, design and construction schedules, and contract specifications for proposed sites or systems.

Evaluate operation of marine equipment during acceptance testing and shakedown cruises. Evaluate data to determine feasibility of product proposals. Analyze project requests to assess the feasibility, productivity, cost, or production time of marine products.

Conduct investigations and hold discussions with clients, contractors, and developers to evaluate needs and modify designs accordingly. Maintain contact with contractors to ensure completion of work at minimum cost.

Establish arrangement of machinery and functional equipment in marine contexts, and oversee installation, operation, maintenance, or repair to ensure systems are installed and function according to specifications. Develop, coordinate, or monitor all aspects of production and maintenance with regards to marine machinery and equipment.

Review work requests and compare them with previous work completed on ships to ensure that costs are economically sound. Schedule machine overhauls and maintain records of engineering department activities.

Inspect marine equipment and machinery to draw up work requests and job specifications. Develop engineering specifications or cost estimates for marine projects, including compliance with environmental regulations or quality standards.

Evaluate performance of craft during dock and sea trials to determine design changes and conformance with safety standards. Coordinate activities with regulatory bodies to ensure repairs and alterations are consistent with safety standards.
Investigate equipment failures and recommend remedial actions. Provide technical consultation or direction related to the development or production of marine systems and components.

Conduct environmental, operational, or performance tests on marine machinery and equipment. Plan or conduct experiments to validate new materials or optimize processes, examining contaminant tolerance and system responses.
Evaluate performance of marine systems and assess needs for modifications or enhancements. Provide technical direction to other engineers or personnel involved in marine projects.
Maintain and coordinate repair of marine machinery and equipment. Act as a liaison between ships' captains and shore personnel to ensure effective operation and maintenance.
Provide advice on materials being used for marine construction, ensuring compliance with quality and environmental standards.

Assist drafters in developing the structural designs of marine products using drafting tools or CAD software. Prepare or direct the preparation of product or system layouts and detailed drawings and schematics for marine applications.
Clean work area.
Operate forklifts to deliver materials.
Program computer numerical control machines.
Refill, change, and monitor the level of fluids, such as oil and coolant, in machines.
Use equipment designed to join sheet metal, such as spot welders.
Repair or replace machine parts, using hand tools, or notify engineering personnel when corrective action is required.
Thread and hand-feed materials through machine cutters or abraders.
Verify conformance of machined work to specifications, using measuring instruments, such as calipers, micrometers, or fixed or telescoping gauges.
Confer with other workers about machine setups and operational specifications.
Repair, maintain, and replace parts on dies.
Set up, operate, or tend presses and forging machines to perform hot or cold forging by flattening, straightening, bending, cutting, piercing, or other operations to taper, shape, or form metal.
Position and move metal wires or workpieces through a series of dies that compress and shape stock to form die impressions.
Install, adjust, and remove dies, synchronizing cams, forging hammers, and stop guides, using overhead cranes or other hoisting devices, and hand tools.
Select, align, and bolt positioning fixtures, stops, and specified dies to rams and anvils, forging rolls, or presses and hammers.
Trim and compress finished forgings to specified tolerances.
Sharpen cutting tools and drill bits, using bench grinders.
Measure completed workpieces to verify conformance to specifications, using micrometers, gauges, calipers, templates, or rulers.
Examine completed workpieces for defects, such as chipped edges or marred surfaces, and sort defective pieces according to types of flaws.
Read work orders or production schedules to determine specifications, such as materials to be used, locations of cutting lines, or dimensions and tolerances.
Load workpieces, plastic material, or chemical solutions into machines.
Maintain stocks of machine parts and machining tools.
Set up, operate, or tend machines to saw, cut, shear, slit, punch, crimp, notch, bend, or straighten metal or plastic material.
Study machining instructions, job orders, or blueprints to determine dimensional or finish specifications, sequences of operations, setups, or tooling requirements.
Select and set cutting speeds, feed rates, depths of cuts, and cutting tools, according to machining instructions or knowledge of metal properties.
Install tools in spindles.
Lay out reference lines and machining locations on work, using layout tools, and applying knowledge of shop math and layout techniques.
Inspect sample workpieces to verify conformance with specifications, using instruments such as gauges, micrometers, and dial indicators.
Verify that workpiece reference lines are parallel to the axis of table rotation, using dial indicators mounted in spindles.
Perform minor assembly, such as fastening parts with nuts, bolts, or screws, using power tools or hand tools.
Start machines, monitor their operations, and record operational data.
Change worn cutting tools, using wrenches.
Observe drilling or boring machine operations to detect any problems.
Establish zero reference points on workpieces, such as at the intersections of two edges or over hole locations.
Operate single- or multiple-spindle drill presses to bore holes so that machining operations can be performed on metal or plastic workpieces.
Turn valves and direct flow of coolants or cutting oil over cutting areas.
Move machine controls to lower tools to workpieces and to engage automatic feeds.
Position and secure workpieces on tables, using bolts, jigs, clamps, shims, or other holding devices.
Adjust air cylinders and setting stops to set traverse lengths and feed arm strokes.
Slide spacers between buffs on spindles to set spacing.
Move controls to set cutting speeds and depths and feed rates, and to position tools in relation to workpieces.
Compute machine indexings and settings for specified dimensions and base reference points.
Plan sequences of operations, applying knowledge of physical properties of workpiece materials.
Turn controls to set cutting speeds, feed rates, or table angles for specified operations.
Mark identifying data on workpieces.
Replace defective blades or wheels, using hand tools.
Lubricate workpieces with oil.
Set stops on machine beds, change dies, and adjust components, such as rams or power presses, when making multiple or successive passes.
Turn valves to start flow of coolant against cutting areas or to start airflow that blows cuttings away from kerfs.
Position guides, stops, holding blocks, or other fixtures to secure and direct workpieces, using hand tools and measuring devices.
Place workpieces on cutting tables, manually or using hoists, cranes, or sledges.
Adjust ram strokes of presses to specified lengths, using hand tools.
Set blade tensions, heights, and angles to perform prescribed cuts, using wrenches.
Scribe reference lines on workpieces as guides for cutting operations, according to blueprints, templates, sample parts, or specifications.
Position, align, and secure workpieces against fixtures or stops on machine beds or on dies.
Clean and lubricate machines.
Install, align, and lock specified punches, dies, cutting blades, or other fixtures in rams or beds of machines, using gauges, templates, feelers, shims, and hand tools.
Grind out burrs or sharp edges, using portable grinders, speed lathes, or polishing jacks.
Sharpen dulled blades, using bench grinders, abrasive wheels, or lathes.
Remove housings, feed tubes, tool holders, or other accessories to replace worn or broken parts, such as springs or bushings.
Measure workpieces and lay out work, using precision measuring devices.
Brush or spray lubricating compounds on workpieces, or turn valve handles and direct flow of coolant against tools and workpieces.
Move machine controls to index workpieces, and to adjust machines for pre-selected operational settings.
Activate machine start-up switches to grind, lap, hone, debar, shear, or cut workpieces, according to specifications.
Mount and position tools in machine chucks, spindles, or other tool holding devices, using hand tools.
Select machine tooling to be used, using knowledge of machine and production requirements.
Set and adjust machine controls according to product specifications, using knowledge of machine operation.
Hone cutters with oilstones to remove nicks.
Observe machine operations to detect any problems, making necessary adjustments to correct problems.
Set up, operate, or tend grinding and related tools that remove excess material or burrs from surfaces, sharpen edges or corners, or buff, hone, or polish metal or plastic workpieces.
Lift and position workpieces, manually or with hoists, and secure them in hoppers or on machine tables, faceplates, or chucks, using clamps.
Inspect or measure finished workpieces to determine conformance to specifications, using measuring instruments, such as gauges or micrometers.
Preheat workpieces, using heating furnaces or hand torches.
Select, clean, and install spacers, rubber sleeves, or cutters on arbors.
Adjust machine controls and change tool settings to keep dimensions within specified tolerances.
Lift workpieces onto work tables either manually or with hoists or direct crane operators to lift and position workpieces.
Start lathe or turning machines and observe operations to ensure that specifications are met.
Verify alignment of workpieces on machines, using measuring instruments such as rules, gauges, or calipers.
Select cutting tools and tooling instructions, according to written specifications or knowledge of metal properties and shop mathematics.
Remove workpieces from machines, and check to ensure that they conform to specifications, using measuring instruments such as microscopes, gauges, calipers, and micrometers.
Observe milling or planing machine operation, and adjust controls to ensure conformance with specified tolerances.
Compute dimensions, tolerances, and angles of workpieces or machines according to specifications and knowledge of metal properties and shop mathematics.
Read work orders or blueprints to determine specified tolerances and sequences of operations for machine setup.
Move cutters or material manually or by turning handwheels, or engage automatic feeding mechanisms to mill workpieces to specifications.
Replace worn tools, using hand tools, and sharpen dull tools, using bench grinders.
Select and install cutting tools and other accessories according to specifications, using hand tools or power tools.
Record production output.
Make templates or cutting tools.
Start machines to produce sample workpieces, and observe operations to detect machine malfunctions and to verify that machine setups conform to specifications.
Compute unspecified dimensions and machine settings, using knowledge of metal properties and shop mathematics.
Turn handles or knobs to set pressures and depths of ram strokes and to synchronize machine operations.
Mount attachments, such as relieving or tracing attachments, to perform operations, such as duplicating contours of templates or trimming workpieces.
Turn valve handles to direct the flow of coolant onto work areas or to coat disks with spinning compounds.
Move toolholders manually or by turning handwheels, or engage automatic feeding mechanisms to feed tools to and along workpieces.
Install holding fixtures, cams, gears, and stops to control stock and tool movement, using hand tools, power tools, and measuring instruments.
Remove dies from machines when production runs are finished.
Position, secure, and align cutting tools in toolholders on machines, using hand tools, and verify their positions with measuring instruments.
Lift metal stock or workpieces manually or using hoists, and position and secure them in machines, using fasteners and hand tools.
Crank machines through cycles, stopping to adjust tool positions and machine controls to ensure specified timing, clearances, and tolerances.
Direct and train other workers to change rolls, operate mill equipment, remove coils and cobbles, and band and load material.
Fill oil cups, adjust valves, and observe gauges to control flow of metal coolants and lubricants onto workpieces.
Record mill production on schedule sheets and monitor inventory levels, ordering supplies as necessary.
Signal and assist other workers to remove and position equipment, fill hoppers, and feed materials into machines.
Activate shears and grinders to trim workpieces, and visually inspect equipment for surface defects.
Calculate draft space and roll speed for each mill stand to plan rolling sequences and dimensions.
Lay out and mark areas of parts to be shot peened and examine electronic components for defects, using microscopes.
Develop, implement, or evaluate maintenance policies and procedures, and perform skilled repair or maintenance operations using hand or power tools.
Set up and operate computer-controlled machines or robots to perform one or more machine functions.
Maintain machines and remove and replace broken or worn machine tools, and adjust machine feed and speed when necessary.
Inspect, test, and measure completed work using precision measuring instruments to verify conformance to specifications.
Examine structures or vehicles using non-destructive testing techniques and document results.
Counsel employees about work-related issues and assist employees in correcting job-skill deficiencies.
Confer with supervisors or programmers to resolve malfunctions or production errors and monitor employees' work levels.
Inspect and monitor work areas, providing safety training to prevent unsafe conditions or violations.
Review program specifications or blueprints to determine and set machine operations and sequencing, and check to ensure workpieces are properly lubricated during operation.
Prepare reports or documentation regarding procedures, such as calibration or results of non-destructive testing methods.
Assist engineers or scientists in the development of new products, tools, fixtures, or processes.
Identify defects in materials using ultrasonic testing techniques and verify part dimensions using precision measuring instruments.
Conduct or arrange for worker training in safety or equipment use and investigate accidents or injuries, preparing reports of findings.

emerging_tasks.task: Use drones for site surveying and to inspect hard-to-reach areas of a structure.
Assemble or inspect hoisting equipment or rigging, such as cables, pulleys, or hooks, to move heavy equipment or materials.
Lift steel beams, girders, or columns using cranes or forklifts, or by signaling hoisting equipment operators to lift or position structural steel members.
Troubleshoot and repair electrical or mechanical equipment.
Use forklifts or cranes to move construction material, such as rebar.
Operate, test, and troubleshoot uncrewed aerial systems, commonly known as drones, to ensure optimal performance.
Program and calibrate drones for specific missions or tasks, ensuring proper functionality and performance.
Inventory and store parts, tools, and equipment.
Troubleshoot electrical, hydraulic, or mechanical equipment and machines.
Communicate results in peer-reviewed research articles or at workshops or conferences.
Develop and test autonomous systems for uncrewed aerospace vehicles.
Develop plans for safely drydocking vessels, including structural integrity, block loading, and stability.
Investigate equipment failures or diagnose faulty operations and recommend or perform remedial actions.
Use agricultural drones for crop monitoring, irrigation management, and pest control.
Operate drones for remote inspection of large or hard-to-reach structures, such as wind turbines, bridges, or tall buildings.
